border-orange-500","children":"A cable nest is not just ugly — it is a daily source of low-level stress and a genuine fire hazard. This guide covers the exact tools, techniques, and order of operations to get your desk cables completely under control."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"why-cable-management-matters","children":"Why Cable Management Matters"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Cables are the enemy of a functional workspace. They create visual noise that impairs focus, they collect dust, they tangle and pull devices off the desk, and — at worst — they create genuine fire hazards when crimped or overloaded."}}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21"]}],"$L22","$L23","$L24"]}],"$L25"]}]]}]
7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L26",null,{"children":["$","$27",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@28"}]}]
9:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The good news: cable management is a one-time project. Spend two hours doing it properly once, and you will not need to revisit it for years."}}]
a:["$","h2","3",{"id":"step-1-go-wireless-where-possible","children":"Step 1: Go Wireless Where Possible"}]
b:["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Before buying cable management products, eliminate as many cables as possible at the source."}}]
c:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The biggest wins:</strong>"}}]
d:["$","ul","6",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Wireless keyboard and mouse</strong>: Removes two cables from the desk surface entirely"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>USB-C monitor with power delivery</strong>: One cable from laptop to monitor replaces the monitor cable plus the laptop power cable"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Wireless charger</strong>: Removes the phone charging cable from the desk"}}]]}]
e:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"After going wireless where practical, the remaining cables are fewer, more manageable, and easier to route cleanly."}}]
f:["$","h2","8",{"id":"step-2-consolidate-with-a-hub-or-dock","children":"Step 2: Consolidate With a Hub or Dock"}]
10:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Multiple individual cables running from devices to laptop is the primary cause of cable chaos. A Thunderbolt 4 or USB-C dock connects your monitor, keyboard, audio, ethernet, and external storage through a single cable to your laptop."}}]
11:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"When you sit down, you connect one cable. When you leave, you disconnect one cable. Everything else stays put. This is the most functional cable management upgrade available."}}]
12:["$","h2","11",{"id":"step-3-route-cables-off-the-desk-surface","children":"Step 3: Route Cables Off the Desk Surface"}]
13:["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Every cable on the desk surface is a cable that clutters the workspace. Route all cables vertically:"}}]
14:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Down the back of monitor arms</strong>: Most monitor arms have cable clips or channels built in. Thread the monitor cable through these before mounting the monitor."}}]
15:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Along desk legs</strong>: Self-adhesive cable clips (£8–12) attach to the inside of desk legs and hold cables flat against the surface, keeping them completely out of sight from the front."}}]
16:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Into a cable spine</strong>: A flexible cable spine runs from the floor power strip up to the desk surface in a single contained channel. Essential for standing desks where the cable length changes with desk height."}}]
17:["$","h2","16",{"id":"step-4-under-desk-cable-tray","children":"Step 4: Under-Desk Cable Tray"}]
18:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Mount a cable tray under the desk to hold the power strip, excess cable length, and any power bricks. The tray attaches with screws or adhesive strips and keeps everything 6–8 inches off the floor — out of sight from seated position and off the floor where it collects dust."}}]
19:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Recommended</strong>: J Channel cable tray (~£15), or the IKEA SIGNUM cable management rail (~£12). Both install in under 20 minutes and immediately transform the under-desk situation."}}]
1a:["$","h2","19",{"id":"step-5-tidy-the-remaining-cables","children":"Step 5: Tidy the Remaining Cables"}]
1b:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Once cables are routed, bundle any excess with velcro cable ties (never zip ties — velcro allows future adjustment). Group cables by function: power cables together, data cables together."}}]
1c:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Label cables with small adhesive labels at both ends. This seems excessive until the first time you need to trace a cable and save 20 minutes of untangling."}}]
1d:["$","h2","22",{"id":"the-ideal-end-state","children":"The Ideal End State"}]
1e:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A fully managed desk has:"}}]
1f:["$","ul","24",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"One cable visible: the single USB-C cable between laptop and monitor/dock"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"No cables on the desk surface"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"No cables visible from the front of the desk"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Power strip mounted under the desk"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"All cable excess hidden in the under-desk tray"}}]]}]
20:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This is achievable on any desk with one afternoon of work. The products required total £40–60. The result lasts indefinitely and the visual and psychological difference is immediate."}}]
21:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For the complete organised workspace picture, see our <a href=\"/blog/home-office-organization-tips\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">home office organisation tips</a>."}}]
22:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium 23:["$","$L29",null,{"title":"Desk Cable Management: How to Eliminate the Cable Nest for Good","slug":"cable-management-desk-guide"}]

28:null
2c:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400 mb-4","children":"Get more workspace ideas in your inbox."}]
